text.title: Definition of Ready Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Definition of Ready Template application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Each of the 7 DoR items MUST be binary yes/no with an observable artefact (link, doc, signoff). Subjective wording is rejected.
rule.rationale: Subjective gates collapse to the loudest voice in planning; binary + observable gates are auditable and consistent across planners.

rule.statement: Acceptance Criteria MUST pass an INVEST-style rubric (Independent / Negotiable / Valuable / Estimable / Small / Testable). ACs failing >=2 rubric items are rejected.
rule.rationale: Sloppy ACs are the leading cause of mid-sprint clarification cycles; the rubric catches them before commit.

rule.statement: UI-touching stories MUST link a design artefact (Figma frame, design doc, accepted mock). Stories without a design link are rejected.
rule.rationale: Dev attempting UI from prose ACs produces design drift and a rework loop; the design link short-circuits it.

rule.statement: Story estimate + remaining sprint capacity MUST add up <= sprint commit. Over-committed sprints are rejected at planning, not absorbed.
rule.rationale: Optimistic capacity commits guarantee mid-sprint cuts; the gate forces the cut to happen before, not during, the sprint.

rule.statement: Each story MUST have a named Product Owner (decision authority) and a named dev owner (technical contact). 'Team' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Diffused decision authority means none; named owners answer the inevitable mid-sprint question without escalation.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
